scores.correctness_notes: Factually correct for what it covers, with precise Python client API details (wait_change, get_change, get_changes, ChangeState, change.ready/err). However, it omits several key gold standard elements: CLI commands (pebble changes / pebble tasks), REST API endpoints (GET /v1/changes/{id}, GET /v1/changes/{id}/wait), the Notices mechanism (change-update notice type), and state persistence to $PEBBLE/.pebble.state. The response is Python-client-centric and misses significant coverage areas.
scores.hallucination_notes: none — all mentioned APIs (wait_change, get_change, get_changes, ChangeState, change.ready, change.err) are real pebble/ops client members. The state table entries (Hold, Undo, Undone) are valid Pebble states not in the gold standard but not fabricated.
